items.title: Rewrite watchlist app in Python
items.body: ## Summary - replace the Java/Maven watchlist implementation with a Python package powered by yfinance - preserve the watchlist CLI options and table-style output while updating README and agent docs - add a small unittest suite for parsing, sorting, and performance helpers ## Testing - python3 -m unittest discover -s tests - python3 -m xscout --tickers=AAPL,MSFT --sort=marketcap --desc

items.title: Rewrite entrypoint in Python; add bulk_create CSV import
items.body:
## Summary

- Rewrites `entrypoint.sh` from bash to Python 3 (filename preserved — Dockerfile `ENTRYPOINT` and Render `startCommand` unchanged). Still shells out to `psql`/`java` so the existing mock-on-PATH test harness keeps working.
- Adds a `bulk_create` action that downloads a CSV from a signed URL and invokes the JAR's existing `create` path once per row. **No JAR modifications.**
- Introduces `PgClient`, `PgConnInfo`, and `BulkSummary` dataclasses; unifies single-action and bulk row arg-building through one `build_args` helper.
- 53 unit tests + 9 integration tests, all green.

## `bulk_create` contract

- `csv_url` (required, HTTPS) serves a CSV with columns `name, email, company, api_key, notes`
- 10 MB body cap (via `Content-Length` or streaming counter), 60s download timeout, 300s per-row JAR timeout (override via `jar_timeout_secs`)
- Emits a `{total, succeeded, failed, errors}` summary on stdout (and to `result_data` JSONB if `correlation_id` + `result_table` are set)
- Exit `0` on any partial success, `2` when `total > 0 && succeeded == 0` — callers can retry without re-running successful rows
- `csv_url` is never logged on any error path (signed URLs may carry credentials)

## Observability / safety improvements addressed in review

- `download_csv` logs `type(e).__name__: str(e)` (not the URL) so ops can distinguish DNS/TLS/refused/404
- `PgClient` methods return `(ok, stderr)` tuples; `_record_result` surfaces psql's actual error text instead of a generic "failed to write"
- Row errors include `stdout[-500:]` when stderr is empty (JAR OOM with silent stderr is now diagnosable)
- Tempdir rmtree failures logged to stderr (don't silently leak `data-sources.xml` containing DB password)
- `ValidationError` paths now construct the `PgClient` best-effort *before* `validate()` runs so validation failures still land in the result table on first run — integration-tested

## CI compatibility

`docker-compose.test.yml` now sets `extra_hosts: host.docker.internal:host-gateway` so the `bulk_create` integration tests (which serve a CSV from the host to the container) work on Linux CI as well as Docker Desktop.

## Test plan

- [x] `uvx pytest` — 53 unit tests green
- [x] `docker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml build && uvx --with psycopg2-binary --with pytest pytest tests/integration/ -v` — 9 integration tests green (includes the bulk_create happy/partial-failure/download-failure paths and the fresh-table validation-error recording case)
- [ ] Smoke test against a Render staging deployment with a real 3-row CSV before rolling to prod

items.title: Merge main into Python branch: port ADSB feed + Konami easter egg
items.body:
`origin/main` had two features land after the Python rewrite branch was cut — a live OpenSky ADSB feed and a Konami code easter egg — causing merge conflicts against the deleted Java files.

## Conflict resolution
- `build.gradle`, `MainController.java` — modified on main, deleted by us → kept deleted
- `AdsbService.java`, `Aircraft.java` — added on main → removed (replaced by Python equivalent)
- `map.html` — auto-merged; Konami easter egg from main, Python backend comments retained

## ADSB feed ported to Python (`src/somethingelse/adsb.py`)
Equivalent of `AdsbService` + `Aircraft` in pure Python using stdlib `urllib` (no new dependency):

```python
aircraft = adsb.fetch_aircraft_in_bounds(
    adsb.UK_LAT_MIN, adsb.UK_LON_MIN,
    adsb.UK_LAT_MAX, adsb.UK_LON_MAX,
)
```

15-second polling runs in a daemon thread, started via `window.events.loaded`.

## Extended JS/Python API
`updateAircraft` gains `heading` and `onGround` parameters. Grounded aircraft render grey; airborne render red.

```js
updateAircraft(icao, lat, lon, callsign, altitude, heading, onGround)
```

items.title: Rewrite COMA algorithm in pure Python
items.body:
## Summary

- **Adds `ComaPy`**, a pure Python implementation of the COMA 3.0 schema matching algorithm, eliminating the Java 6 runtime dependency (40MB JAR + subprocess IPC)
- Faithfully reproduces COMA's matching pipeline: trigram (Dice) name/path similarity, TF-IDF instance matching with global IDF (matching Lucene 3.x `sqrt(tf) * log(N/df)`), and structural matchers (leaves, parents) — all combined via max-matching Dice set aggregation
- Supports `use_schema`, `use_instances`, `delta`, `threshold`, and `max_n` parameters for full control over matching behavior
- **Deprecates the Java `Coma` class** with a `DeprecationWarning`; `ComaPy` will be renamed to `Coma` in valentine v1.0.0
- Adds 68 tests: regression tests on two datasets (easy + hard with different column names/abbreviations) and unit tests for all internal modules (combination, selection, trigram, TF-IDF, schema graph, datatype similarity)
- Updates README with `ComaPy` documentation and deprecation notice for Java `Coma`

items.title: auto: convert hello.py to Java
items.body:
Converts `hello.py` to an equivalent Java implementation under `java_translation/Hello.java` as required by the automated Python-to-Java workflow.

## Changes

- **New file: `java_translation/Hello.java`**
  - `greet(String name)` → string concatenation equivalent of the f-string
  - `add(int a, int b)` → direct integer addition
  - `fibonacci(int n)` → iterative `List<Integer>` implementation matching Python's list output
  - `main` reproduces identical console output to the Python `__main__` block

```java
public static List<Integer> fibonacci(int n) {
    if (n <= 0) return new ArrayList<>();
    if (n == 1) { ... }
    List<Integer> fib = new ArrayList<>(List.of(0, 1));
    for (int i = 2; i < n; i++)
        fib.add(fib.get(i - 1) + fib.get(i - 2));
    return fib;
}
```

Original `hello.py` is untouched.

items.title: Add Java translation of hello.py
items.body:
Converts `hello.py` to idiomatic Java as required, placing the output in `java_translation/Hello.java`.

## Changes

- **`java_translation/Hello.java`** — New file. Translates three functions and the main entry point:
  - `greet(String name)` → string concatenation
  - `add(int a, int b)` → integer addition
  - `fibonacci(int n)` → iterative `List<Integer>` build, matching Python semantics (empty list for `n≤0`, `[0]` for `n=1`)

```java
public static List<Integer> fibonacci(int n) {
    if (n <= 0) return new ArrayList<>();
    if (n == 1) { ... return List([0]); }
    List<Integer> fib = new ArrayList<>(List.of(0, 1));
    for (int i = 2; i < n; i++)
        fib.add(fib.get(i - 1) + fib.get(i - 2));
    return fib;
}
```

Original `hello.py` is untouched.

items.title: Port PCTSEA Java tool to Python CLI application with full tests
items.body:
This pull request introduces the initial implementation of the PCTSEA (Proteomics Cell Type Single-cell Enrichment Analysis) Python package. It adds the core CLI, configuration, cell type ontology logic, and project setup necessary for running the analysis pipeline. The most important changes are grouped below.

**Project setup and configuration:**

* Added `pyproject.toml` with project metadata, dependencies, build configuration, and development tools, ensuring the package is ready for installation and development. (`pctsea-python/pyproject.toml`)
* Added `.gitignore` and `.python-version` files to manage Python environment and versioning. (`pctsea-python/.gitignore`, `.python-version`) [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-207e4ab656403ee1ebd790cd2dd77356efba7b6ded15b49bd92fc5c82f94ada2R1-R8) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-02b16bdc82ea3d804c824a6ff312f09397e04f687fdc2b1167df94d7a601763eR1)

**Core package and CLI:**

* Implemented the main CLI entry point in `cli.py`, enabling users to run the PCTSEA pipeline with various options for scoring, thresholds, database connection, and output. (`pctsea-python/src/pctsea/cli.py`)
* Added package initialization and versioning in `__init__.py`. (`pctsea-python/src/pctsea/__init__.py`)

**Cell type ontology and configuration:**

* Implemented hierarchical cell type mapping, typo correction, and normalization logic in `cell_types.py`, supporting robust cell type resolution and classification. (`pctsea-python/src/pctsea/cell_types.py`)
* Defined enums and constants for scoring methods, cell type hierarchy, and input data types in `config.py`, centralizing configuration for the analysis pipeline. (`pctsea-python/src/pctsea/config.py`)

items.title: Convert MIMIR to a clean Python-only codebase
items.body:
### Motivation
- Remove the legacy Java/Maven monolith and streamline the repository around the active Python services so the project is clearly Python-first and easier to maintain.
- Update docs and runtime naming to avoid confusion about Java-era artifacts and to present the current two-service architecture (SIP Flow Handler + Media Bridge).

### Description
- Removed the Java source tree and Maven build file (`src/` and `pom.xml`) and deleted legacy Java tests/resources to make the repository Python-only.
- Rewrote the top-level `README.md` to present Python setup, local quickstart, and Docker Compose usage, and simplified `services/README.md` to reflect the Python service architecture and controller contracts.
- Renamed and refactored media backend identifiers and classes in `services/media-bridge/app/backends.py` to Python-native names and defaults (`python` / `python-optimized`) and simplified backend routing logic.
- Kept and adjusted the Python services under `services/` and ensured no in-process coupling remains between SIP handler and media bridge (contract-driven API/event model).

### Testing
- Ran `python -m compileall services` to byte-compile the service packages and it completed successfully.
- Verified no Java/Maven artifacts remain using `rg --files | rg '(^pom\.xml$|^src/|\.java$)'`, which returned no results indicating the Java implementation was removed.
- No other automated tests were run as part of this change; the repository now exposes the Python FastAPI services which can be exercised with the documented local quickstart and `docker compose up`.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_e_69d39fbe7ef4832295931eca7c92ed4f)

items.title: Convert Python ISO 20022 address converter to Java
items.body:
## Summary

- **Full port** of the Python ISO 20022 address converter to a Java 17 Maven project under `java/`
- Converts all modules: CLI entry point, libpostal parsing (via jpostal JNI), Nominatim geocoding enrichment, PostalAddress24 XML generation, XSD validation, and CSV output
- Replaces Python dependencies with Java equivalents: jpostal, nv-i18n, commons-csv, Jackson, SLF4J/Logback

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify Maven build compiles successfully (`mvn clean compile`)
- [ ] Install libpostal + jpostal JNI bindings and verify address parsing works
- [ ] Run with a sample address file and compare CSV output against the Python version
- [ ] Validate generated XML against both structured and hybrid XSD schemas
- [ ] Test geo-enrichment flow with Nominatim for missing town/country

items.title: Java 8 → Java 21 upgrade
items.body: ## Summary Upgrades the Eureka codebase from Java 8 (source/target 1.8) to Java 21. This includes updating `sourceCompatibility`/`targetCompatibility` to 21 across all subprojects, upgrading 15+ dependencies to their latest stable Java 21-compatible versions, fixing all compilation errors from API changes in Mockito 5.x, MockServer 5.x, and the Jetty 6→9 migration, and adding `--add-opens` JVM flags required by Java 21's strong encapsulation for reflection-heavy test libraries. ### Updates since last revision - Fixed missing `baseRequest.setHandled(true)` in the `simulateNotReady` path of `eureka-core`'s `MockRemoteEurekaServer`. Without this, Jetty 9 would override the intended 403 Forbidden response with a 404 Not Found, since the request was never marked as handled and the response was not committed. (Addresses Devin Review feedback.) ## Java Version Changes | File | Declaration | Before | After | |------|-------------|--------|-------| | `build.gradle` | `sourceCompatibility` | `1.8` | `21` | | `build.gradle` | `targetCompatibility` | `1.8` | `21` | | `build.gradle` | IDEA `languageLevel` | `'1.8'` | `'21'` | | `README.md` | Build requirement | `java8` | `java21` | These declarations are in the root `build.gradle` `subprojects` block and apply to all 10 subprojects. ## Dependencies Updated | Library | Old Version | New Version | Reason | |---------|------------|-------------|--------| | Jackson (core, annotations, databind) | 2.10.5 / 2.10.5.1 | 2.18.3 | Latest stable; full Java 21 module support | | Woodstox Core | 5.2.1 | 7.1.0 | Latest stable; Java 21 compatible StAX impl | | Google Guice | 4.1.0 | 5.1.0 | Latest stable; Java 21 reflection compatibility | | AWS SDK (core, ec2, autoscaling, sts, route53) | 1.11.277 | 1.12.782 | Latest 1.x stable | | Apache HttpClient | 4.5.3 | 4.5.14 | Latest 4.x stable; security fixes | | Jersey 1.x | 1.19.1 | 1.19.4 | Latest 1.x stable | | XStream | 1.4.19 | 1.4.21 | Latest stable; security fixes | | Archaius Core | 0.7.6 | 0.7.7 | Latest stable | | Gretty (Gradle plugin) | 3.1.0 | 4.1.6 | Latest stable; Java 21 support | | JUnit 4 | 4.11 | 4.13.2 | Latest 4.x stable | | Mockito | 3.4.0 | 5.15.2 | Latest stable; native Java 21 support | | MockServer (netty + junit-rule) | 3.9.2 | 5.15.0 | Latest stable; Java 21 compatible | | Jetty (test infra) | 7.2.0 (Mortbay) | 9.4.57 (Eclipse) | Mortbay EOL; 9.4 is latest javax.servlet-compatible | | WireMock | 2.25.1 (`wiremock-jre8`) | 3.12.1 (`wiremock-standalone`) | Latest stable; standalone avoids Jetty 11 conflict | | AssertJ | 3.11.1 | 3.27.3 | Latest stable | | javax.annotation-api | _(not present)_ | 1.3.2 | **Added**: `@PostConstruct`/`@PreDestroy` removed from JDK in Java 11 | | mockserver-junit-rule | _(not present)_ | 5.15.0 | **Added**: `MockServerRule` split to separate artifact in 5.x | ## Breaking Changes Resolved 1. **`@PostConstruct`/`@PreDestroy` removed from JDK (Java 11+)**: Added `javax.annotation-api:1.3.2` to `eureka-client`, `eureka-core`, `eureka-examples`. 2. **Mockito 5.x removed `org.mockito.Matchers`**: Changed all imports to `org.mockito.ArgumentMatchers`; replaced deprecated `anyVararg()` with `any()`. ~15 test files affected. 3. **Mockito 5.x moved `MockitoJUnitRunner`**: `org.mockito.runners` → `org.mockito.junit` package. 4. **Mockito 5.x merged `mockito-inline` into `mockito-core`**: Replaced dependency in `eureka-client/build.gradle`. 5. **MockServer 5.x package restructure**: `org.mockserver.client.server` → `org.mockserver.client`; `MockServerRule` extracted to separate `mockserver-junit-rule` artifact; `getHttpPort()` renamed to `getPort()`. 6. **Jetty 6 (Mortbay) → Jetty 9 (Eclipse) migration**: Rewrote both `MockRemoteEurekaServer` test utilities — new imports (`org.eclipse.jetty.*`), new `AbstractHandler.handle()` signature (adds `Request baseRequest` param), `ServerConnector.getLocalPort()` API, `setHandler()` instead of `addHandler()`. Also required adding `baseRequest.setHandled(true)` in early-return paths (e.g. `simulateNotReady`) to prevent Jetty 9 from overriding response status codes. 7. **WireMock 3.x / Jetty 11 classpath conflict**: WireMock 3.x transitively brings Jetty 11 (`jakarta.servlet`), which overrode our Jetty 9 (`javax.servlet`) on the classpath. Fixed by using `wiremock-standalone` (fat JAR with shaded deps). 8. **Jetty 6 `ServletHandler` filter pattern removed in Jetty 9**: `eureka-core` `MockRemoteEurekaServer` constructor previously registered `ServerRequestAuthFilter` and `RateLimitingFilter` via Jetty 6's `ServletHandler`. This was removed because Jetty 9's `AbstractHandler` doesn't support inline filter chains. Auth header assertions remain. 9. **`Date.format()` in JAR manifest**: Replaced with `java.time.OffsetDateTime.now().format(...)`. 10. **Gradle `mainClassName` deprecated**: Updated to `mainClass` in `eureka-examples/build.gradle`. ## JVM Flag Requirements 10 `--add-opens` flags added to `build.gradle` test JVM args for reflection-based libraries (Mockito/ByteBuddy, XStream, Archaius, Jersey): | Flag | Reason | |------|--------| | `java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED` | Mockito/ByteBuddy mock generation | | `java.base/java.lang.reflect=ALL-UNNAMED` | Mockito reflection internals | | `java.base/java.util=ALL-UNNAMED` | XStream/Archaius collection reflection | | `java.base/java.util.concurrent=ALL-UNNAMED` | Archaius/Governator concurrent utils | | `java.base/java.net=ALL-UNNAMED` | Eureka HTTP client test reflection | | `java.base/java.io=ALL-UNNAMED` | XStream I/O class reflection | | `java.base/java.math=ALL-UNNAMED` | Jackson/XStream BigDecimal serialization | | `java.base/java.text=ALL-UNNAMED` | Date/time formatting reflection | | `java.base/sun.security.ssl=ALL-UNNAMED` | Jersey/Apache HttpClient SSL internals | | `java.base/sun.net.www.protocol.https=ALL-UNNAMED` | Jersey HTTPS protocol handler | **⚠️ Technical Debt**: These bypass Java 21's module encapsulation. They should be resolved by upgrading to library versions with proper module-aware APIs, or migrating away from reflection-heavy libraries (e.g., XStream → Jackson). ## Known Issues / Remaining Work 1. **16 test failures remain** — investigation suggests these are pre-existing (see Test Results). The 7 `EurekaClientServerRestIntegrationTest` failures and `EurekaClientLifecycleTest`/`EurekaClientLifecycleServerFailureTest` failures should be verified against master. 2. **Filter removal in `eureka-core` `MockRemoteEurekaServer`** — `ServerRequestAuthFilter` and `RateLimitingFilter` are no longer exercised in tests. Should be re-added using Jetty 9's `ServletContextHandler` with proper `FilterHolder` registration. 3. **`System.runFinalization()` deprecated for removal** — `StringCacheTest.java` still calls this; should be addressed in a follow-up. 4. **`javax.servlet` → `jakarta.servlet` migration not performed** — The codebase still uses `javax.servlet`. Jetty 9.4 (latest javax-compatible) is used; a future Jetty 10+/11+ upgrade would require this migration. ## Test Results | Metric | Before (Java 8 baseline*) | After (Java 21) | |--------|--------------------------|------------------| | **Total tests** | 279 | 434 | | **Passed** | 233 | 414 | | **Failed** | 42 | 16 | | **Skipped** | 4 | 4 | \* *Before count covers only `eureka-client` + `eureka-client-archaius2` (other modules weren't runnable). After count covers all modules.* **Module-level breakdown (after):** | Module | Tests | Passed | Failed | Skipped | |--------|-------|--------|--------|---------| | eureka-client | 279 | 266 | 9 | 4 | | eureka-client-archaius2 | 5 | 5 | 0 | 0 | | eureka-client-jersey2 | 17 | 17 | 0 | 0 | | eureka-core | 119 | 119 | 0 | 0 | | eureka-core-jersey2 | 7 | 7 | 0 | 0 | | eureka-server | 7 | 0 | 7 | 0 | ## Review & Testing Checklist for Human - [ ] **Validate the 16 test failures are pre-existing, not regressions.** Run the test suite on `master` with Java 21 to confirm these same tests also fail there. The 7 `EurekaClientServerRestIntegrationTest` failures and `EurekaClientLifecycleTest`/`EurekaClientLifecycleServerFailureTest` failures should be investigated. - [ ] **Verify the filter removal in `eureka-core/.../MockRemoteEurekaServer.java` is acceptable.** The constructor previously registered `ServerRequestAuthFilter` and `RateLimitingFilter` via Jetty 6's `ServletHandler`. These were removed entirely because Jetty 9's `AbstractHandler` doesn't support inline filter chains. Auth header assertions remain, but the filters themselves are no longer exercised. This could reduce meaningful test coverage for rate limiting and auth behavior. Consider re-implementing with `ServletContextHandler` + `FilterHolder`. - [ ] **Audit the 10 `--add-opens` flags.** Two flags open `sun.*` internal packages (`sun.security.ssl`, `sun.net.www.protocol.https`). These were added broadly rather than being individually verified as necessary. Consider running tests without each flag individually to find the minimal required set. - [ ] **Confirm `wiremock-standalone` (fat JAR) doesn't introduce classpath conflicts.** The shaded JAR bundles its own Jetty 11 internally. Verify no shaded classes leak onto the classpath and conflict with Jetty 9 or other dependencies. - [ ] **Assess downstream impact of `sourceCompatibility = 21`.** Compiled artifacts now require Java 21+. Consumers previously on Java 8+ will need to upgrade. **Suggested test plan:** `./gradlew compileJava compileTestJava` to verify compilation, then `./gradlew test` to verify the test suite. Specifically check that eureka-core (119/119 pass) and eureka-client-jersey2 (17/17 pass) remain green. ### Notes - The `Date.format()` → `java.time.OffsetDateTime` change in `build.gradle`'s JAR manifest produces ISO-8601 timestamps with `+00:00` instead of `+0000`. Cosmetic only. - `mockito-inline` replaced with `mockito-core` — Mockito 5.x made inline mocking the default. - `mainClassName` → `mainClass` in eureka-examples fixes Gradle deprecation warnings. - The "before" test count is not directly comparable to "after" because additional modules (`eureka-core`, `eureka-core-jersey2`, `eureka-server`, `eureka-client-jersey2`) now compile and run tests that previously couldn't execute. Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/e19b8d9cea354b3e82fa24d833a275ef Requested by: @iancarter212-personal ---

items.title: Refactor AnsibleModule to Java implementation
items.body:
This PR moves the `AnsibleModule` implementation from Python (`ansible_bridge.py`) to Java (`PythonAnsibleModuleMock.java`). This refactoring improves the maintainability and performance of the module mock by utilizing Java's native capabilities for file system operations and hashing, while maintaining the necessary Python interface for Ansible module compatibility. Key changes include:
- A new `PythonAnsibleModuleMock` class in Java that implements parameter specification parsing, `run_command` delegation, and various hashing and file attribute methods.
- Injection of a `Factory` for this mock into the GraalPy context via `TaskExecutor`.
- Delegation of `AnsibleModule` logic in `ansible_bridge.py` to the Java-backed mock.
- Improved stability by using `_deep_convert` to normalize complex types before they cross the Java-Python boundary.

---
*PR created automatically by Jules for task [3831848965427245340](https://jules.google.com/task/3831848965427245340) started by @yamanakahirofumi*

items.title: Convert runnable examples to Python and remove redundant non-Python coverage
items.body:
This updates `example/` so the runnable examples are Python-first and removes non-Python examples whose concepts were already covered elsewhere. The remaining example set keeps distinct transport/feature coverage without duplicating basic HTTP flows.

- **Example set reshaped**
  - Replaced non-Python runnable examples with Python equivalents:
    - `py-binary`
    - `py-http-cookie-auth`
    - `py-websocket`
  - Removed `java-http-basic` because basic HTTP request/reply is already demonstrated by existing Python examples.

- **Python ports**
  - Reimplemented the binary negotiation example using the Python `Client`/`Server` path.
  - Reimplemented the cookie-to-`@auth_` HTTP boundary example using Python HTTP server glue plus `update_headers`.
  - Reimplemented the WebSocket request/reply example in Python using `websockets`.

- **Example surface cleanup**
  - Updated `example/Makefile` and `example/README.md` to reference only the Python example set.
  - Updated docs that pointed at removed/renamed examples to point at the new Python examples.
  - Removed workflow cache references tied only to the deleted TypeScript example.

Example of the new Python transport boundary pattern:

```python
def update_headers(headers: dict[str, object]) -> None:
    if session_token is not None:
        headers['@auth_'] = {'sessionToken': session_token}

response = asyncio.run(telepact_server.process(request_bytes, update_headers))
```

items.title: #00-Java
items.body:
## Describe tus cambios
Se sube el ejercicio 00
***(Opcional)*** *Sobre todo aconsejable si la "Pull Request" se corresponde con una corrección adicional y no con la presentación de un ejercicio.*

## Comprobaciones
Asegúrate de cumplir los siguientes puntos antes de realizar la "Pull Request":

- [ ] El título de mi Pull Request sigue este formato: "#[número] - [lenguaje_utilizado]". *(Ej: #00 - Python")*
- [ ] El nombre el fichero se corresponde con el de mi usuario en GitHub más la extensión del lenguaje. *(Ej: mouredev.py)*
- [ ] El fichero de corrección se encuentra dentro del directorio del ejercicio y en una carpeta con el nombre del lenguaje de programación utilizado en minúsculas. *(Ej: Roadmap/00 - SINTAXIS, VARIABLES, TIPOS DE DATOS Y HOLA MUNDO/python/mouredev.py)*
- [ ] He revisado que el nombre del directorio del lenguaje no es conflictivo:
	- c#, no csharp
	- c++, no cplusplus
	- go, no golang
	- javascript, no js
- [ ] Únicamente he incluido los ficheros de ejercicios. No se aceptarán Pull Requests que contengan archivos adicionales asociados a editores de código o semejantes.

## Información

* Tienes toda la información sobre los retos semanales en [retosdeprogramacion.com/roadmap](https://retosdeprogramacion.com/roadmap).
* Recuerda que tienes un grupo de apoyo llamado "ejercicios-logica" en [Discord](https://discord.gg/mouredev).

items.title: Migrate from Java 7 to Java 11 LTS
items.body:
The entire codebase targeted Java 1.7 with obsolete JVM flags, removed APIs, and backport libraries that are unnecessary or incompatible with modern JDKs. This migrates everything to Java 11 LTS.

### Build configuration
- `source`/`target` `1.7` → `11` in all 8 `build.xml` files (including yguard variants)
- Removed `bootclasspath` referencing `rt.jar`, `jce.jar`, `jsr166-1.7.0.jar` — these don't exist in the module system (Java 9+)
- Eclipse `.settings/org.eclipse.jdt.core.prefs` compliance updated to 11

### Startup scripts (12 files)
- Removed `-XX:-UseSplitVerifier` (removed in Java 9)
- Removed CMS GC flags (`-XX:+UseParNewGC`, `-XX:+CMSParallelRemarkEnabled`, `-XX:+UseConcMarkSweepGC`) — removed in Java 14, G1GC is default since Java 9
- Removed `-Xbootclasspath/p:` (unsupported in Java 9+)
- `JAVAVER=1.7` → `JAVAVER=11`
- Added `--add-opens java.base/sun.misc=ALL-UNNAMED` for `sun.misc.Unsafe` access in chmv8 internals

### JAXB (removed from JDK 11)
- Added external `jaxb-api-2.3.1`, `jaxb-impl-2.3.9`, `javax.activation-api-1.2.0`, `istack-commons-runtime-3.0.12` to all module `libs/` directories
- Updated `.classpath` files; removed stale `jsr166-1.7.0.jar` entries

### Replace chmv8 backport with `java.util.concurrent` (17 files)
The `chmv8` package was a Java 7 backport of Java 8's `ForkJoinPool` and `ConcurrentHashMap`. With Java 11, the standard library equivalents are used directly:

```java
// Before
import com.aionemu.commons.utils.internal.chmv8.ForkJoinPool;
import com.aionemu.commons.utils.internal.chmv8.PlatformDependent;
Map<K, V> map = PlatformDependent.newConcurrentHashMap();

// After
import java.util.concurrent.ForkJoinPool;
import java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentHashMap;
Map<K, V> map = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
```

Affected classes: `ForEach`, `BufferUtils`, `WalkerFormationsCache`, `WorldWalkerFormations`, `HousingService`, `WorkStealThreadFactory`, `ThreadPoolManager`, `ChatService`, `BroadcastService`, `ChatClient` — across AL-Game, AL-Game-5.8, and AL-Chat.

### Note
The `chmv8` package source files in `AL-Commons` are retained since they still compile and may be referenced by other downstream code. They can be removed in a follow-up once confirmed unused.

items.title: chore: upgrade Java 21 to Java 25
items.body:
Upgrades the project from Java 21 to Java 25.

## Changes
- `build.gradle.kts`: Updated `JvmTarget.JVM_21` → `JvmTarget.JVM_25` and `JavaVersion.VERSION_21` → `JavaVersion.VERSION_25`
- `Dockerfile`: Updated base image from `java-chainguard-21` to `java-chainguard-25`
- `.github/workflows/build-and-deploy.yml`: Updated `javaversion: 21` → `javaversion: 25` for all three jobs (`test`, `codeql`, `build`)
- `README.md`: Updated Java prerequisite from Java 17 to Java 25

items.title: feat(sdk): SpaceClient for Java + Node, Java keepAlive
items.body:
## Summary
- **Java SpaceClient** — write, read, take, contents, notify, renewTuple, cancelTuple. Records-based API matching Go SDK surface.
- **Java LeaseClient.keepAlive** — background `ScheduledExecutorService` renewal at TTL/2, returns `Closeable` handle.
- **Node SpaceClient** — write, read, take, contents, notify (raw stream), watch (async generator), renewTuple, cancelTuple.
- **Node proto stubs** — added space.proto, transaction.proto, event.proto to the generation command.
- Both DjinnClients now expose `space()` with a channel on `:9006`.

Unblocks the Auction House polyglot demo — Java can now write/read/take tuples, Node can watch them with SSE.

## Test plan
- [x] `cargo clippy` — clean
- [x] `cargo fmt --check` — clean  
- [x] `cargo test --all` — pass
- [x] `go build/vet` (cli + sdk) — clean
- [x] `./gradlew build` — clean
- [x] `npm run build` (node sdk) — clean

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
